description_long: This structure is a traditional pagoda with a multi-tiered, dark blue roof accented by gold trim and a prominent ornamental finial at the apex. The roof features three distinct levels, each with pronounced, upward-curving eaves, giving the building an elegant, layered silhouette. The main body of the pagoda is open on the sides, supported by slender columns, and features a raised wooden platform with steps leading up to it. The base is rectangular, with a central walkway and a small set of stairs, suggesting an entrance or ceremonial approach. The overall design is symmetrical, with decorative elements that evoke East Asian architectural motifs. The materials appear to be primarily wood and tile, with metallic or gilded details on the roof and finial.
description: A multi-tiered East Asian pagoda with blue, gold-trimmed, upward-curving roofs and an ornate finial, set on a raised wooden platform with open sides.
description_view_0: The pagoda is seen from above and slightly to the front, highlighting the layered roof, gold finial, and the central walkway with stairs leading up to the platform.
description_view_1: This side view shows the pagoda partially disassembled, with the roof, platform, and finial separated, revealing the construction layers and the open structure beneath the roof.
description_view_2: A direct front view emphasizes the symmetry of the pagoda, the three stacked roof tiers, and the open entrance with steps leading up to the platform.
description_view_3: The rear side view again shows the pagoda in a partially exploded state, with the roof and platform components separated, and the gold finial clearly visible.
